frameworks.name: sensory recruitment model
frameworks.one_line: precise visual details maintained in stimulus-selective regions that perceive them

frameworks.name: inverted encoding model
frameworks.one_line: quantitative channel-based reconstruction of VWM contents from fMRI activity

core_question: How do visual working memory representations persist through distracting visual input across visual cortex and IPS?
key_assumptions: VWM contents can be read out as channel-tuned reconstructions from fMRI
early visual cortex and IPS both encode item-specific orientation
distractors share representational format with maintained items
manipulations: presence vs absence of distractors during VWM delay
ROI: early visual cortex vs intraparietal sulcus
key_findings: orientation information held simultaneously in early visual cortex and IPS in anticipation of distraction
representations persisted in both regions when no distractor appeared
visual-cortex reconstructions were biased toward the distractor; IPS reconstructions were not
behavioral reports showed a small attractive bias toward distractors
interpretations: early visual representations are susceptible to interference while IPS is robust
a dynamic visual-parietal tradeoff supports flexible adaptation to task demands
